topic: A close-up of a 'Vinyl Record Player' being started.

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1 [00:00-00:02]: Power on the turntable — front view
Shot 2 [00:02-00:05]: Platter spins up with the record — close-up
Shot 3 [00:05-00:08]: Tonearm lowers and playback begins — side-angle
parsed_generation.narration: The sequence follows a vinyl record player as it powers up, the platter begins to spin with the record in motion, and the tonearm lowers to start playback.

feedback:
Compared to the gold standard, the sequence should begin with a macro close-up of the record on the platter and a hand lowering the tonearm, followed by the needle entering the groove and a brief crackle. The current output starts with a front-view power-on shot and lacks the key close-up hand action and groove-contact moment. To fix, reframe as [00:00-00:04] macro shot of the record on the platter as it begins to spin, [00:04-00:08] show the tonearm lowering into the groove with the needle contacting the record (and a subtle crackle SFX) before the music starts.
Failed checks: topic_fidelity, narration_adds_context
strengths: - Clear three-shot structure with time stamps
fixes: - Replace the opening with a macro close-up of the record on the platter
- Add a shot of the tonearm lowering and the needle contacting the groove, including a crackle/scratch sound cue
